  1. de.fhdwhannover.integrea.ga.core

    1. loadData(List)

    5. getPreview(Individual)

Schnittstelle GAProblem<T>

Typparameter:
T - The gene type used in the chromosomes of the population (e.g., Integer, Character).
Alle bekannten Implementierungsklassen:
CryptanalysisProblem, KnapsackProblem, SudokuProblem, SymRegProblem, TSPProblem
public interface GAProblem<T>
Interface representing a generic Genetic Algorithm Problem Domain.

Implementing classes manage problem-specific initialization (e.g., parsing data to configure fitness landscapes or constraints), mapping configurations to a fitness function, and generating the initial populations.

Implementation Example: 

public class MyCustomProblem implements GAProblem<Double> {

    private MyFitnessFunction fitnessFunction;

    @Override
    public void loadData(List<String[]> rawData) {
        // Parse data and initialize constraints here
        this.fitnessFunction = new MyFitnessFunction(parsedData);
    }

    @Override
    public FitnessFunction<Double> getFitnessFunction() {
        return this.fitnessFunction;
    }

    @Override
    public Population<Double> getInitialPopulation(int size) {
        // Return a starting population of randomized individuals
        return new Population<>(generatedIndividuals);
    }
}

  • Methodenübersicht

    Modifikator und Typ
    Methode
    Beschreibung
    FitnessFunction<T>
    getFitnessFunction()
    Retrieves the strictly configured fitness function representing the mathematical goal of this specific problem domain.
    Population<T>
    getInitialPopulation(int populationSize)
    Generates a fully randomized initial population of the required data structure to seed generation 0.
    default String
    getPreview(Individual<T> individual)
    Translates a deeply nested chromosome back into a short, human-readable preview string.
    void
    loadData(List<String[]> rawData)
    Loads raw CSV or text data specific to the problem domain.
    default void
    printSolution(Individual<T> bestIndividual)
    Output hook allowing the problem to dictate how its complex solution structure should be printed or exported at the culmination of the evolutionary loop.

  • Methodendetails

    • loadData

      void loadData(List<String[]> rawData)
      Loads raw CSV or text data specific to the problem domain. The input is provided as a list of string arrays, typical of CSV output. Implementing classes must parse the data (e.g., city coordinates for TSP, item weights for Knapsack, raw cipher text for Cryptanalysis) and configure the problem state accordingly.

      If the data is empty or malformed, a RuntimeException should be thrown.

      Parameter:
      rawData - A list containing the rows parsed from a data source.
      Löst aus:
      RuntimeException - if the dataset is empty or invalid.

    • getFitnessFunction

      FitnessFunction<T> getFitnessFunction()
      Retrieves the strictly configured fitness function representing the mathematical goal of this specific problem domain.

      Constraint: Must return a valid FitnessFunction<T> initialized with the specific constraints mapped from loadData. Returning a null evaluator will crash the algorithm upon the first evolutionary evaluation pass.

      Gibt zurück:
      The properly instantiated FitnessFunction evaluator.

    • getInitialPopulation

      Population<T> getInitialPopulation(int populationSize)
      Generates a fully randomized initial population of the required data structure to seed generation 0.

      Constraint: The implementation must generate exactly populationSize valid individuals. Each individual must possess chromosomes built within the strict mathematical boundaries of the problem (e.g., Sudoku must preserve fixed cells, TSP must generate a valid permutation containing all cities).

      Parameter:
      populationSize - The requested integer size of the population to generate. Must be > 0.
      Gibt zurück:
      The newly initialized Population<T> containing valid structural permutations.

    • printSolution

      default void printSolution(Individual<T> bestIndividual)
      Output hook allowing the problem to dictate how its complex solution structure should be printed or exported at the culmination of the evolutionary loop.

      Constraint: The method must gracefully handle potentially incomplete individuals and cleanly output formatting over standard I/O (like a 2D text grid for Sudoku).

      Parameter:
      bestIndividual - The absolute fittest Individual extracted from the final generation.

    • getPreview

      default String getPreview(Individual<T> individual)
      Translates a deeply nested chromosome back into a short, human-readable preview string.

      Used exclusively to build the single-line telemetry output emitted every N generations. Constraint: This formatting logic must be extremely light. It must not throw StackOverflowErrors when resolving deeply nested trees (like in Symbolic Regression). It should return a condensed subset if the decoded structure exceeds console limits.

      Parameter:
      individual - The targeted individual to unpack and preview.
      Gibt zurück:
      A short, robust String explicitly denoting the individual's evaluated genetic state.
